items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

image.altText: Two women sit opposite each other.

items.title: Daly River service providers facing massive clean-up bill after record flood

children.content: Residents of the Northern Territory town of Nauiyu / Daly River have started returning home after spending three months in evacuation centres following extensive flooding.

image.altText: A man in a bright orange shirt sprays the muddy blue wall of a house

items.title: Country's biggest barra fishing comp under way, with some reporting more crocs

children.content: The country's biggest annual barramundi fishing competition is under way on the Northern Territory's remote Daly River, after the tournament was delayed by six weeks due to extensive flooding.

gallery.altText: White man standing on boat, wearing long sleeve purple / navy hoodie, holding barramundi fish from belly and mouth, posing
Foreground are broken tree branches, piles of mud, with a small fishing black fishing boat with two people in the background.
A brown crocodile sitting on the muddy banks of a river, tale slightly curved around.

thumbnail.altText: White man standing on boat, wearing long sleeve purple / navy hoodie, holding barramundi fish from belly and mouth, posing
Foreground are broken tree branches, piles of mud, with a small fishing black fishing boat with two people in the background.
A brown crocodile sitting on the muddy banks of a river, tale slightly curved around.

items.title: NT Supreme Court clears way for potential wave of summary adjournments

children.content: The Northern Territory Supreme Court has cleared the way for criminal defendants refused legal aid on funding grounds to have their cases adjourned indefinitely by overturning its own two-decade old ruling.

image.altText: a woman wearing glasses and a paisley dress sitting in a dark room

items.title: Cost-of-living pressure 'entrenching disadvantage' in the NT

children.content: The NT has the second highest average rent in the country, with households having to set aside more of their weekly wages to cover essential costs.

image.altText: Aerial shot of houses in Darwin

items.title: Under-fire CEO defends Alice Springs camp 'squalor' after Kumanjayi Little Baby's death

children.content: After the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the state of Alice Springs's town camps shocked the nation. Now, the man in charge of servicing them is speaking out.

gallery.altText: A love heart is drawn on a road into some gates.

thumbnail.altText: A love heart is drawn on a road into some gates.

items.title: Tour operators brace for Kakadu site closures 'like COVID all over again'

children.content: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

image.altText: An aerial shot of Jim Jim Falls

items.title: Dramatic daylight arrest of NT fugitive, with police shooting car tyre

children.content: Northern Territory police have carried out a dramatic arrest of a 33-year-old man on a main Darwin road, saying the fugitive was wanted for a breach of parole.

image.altText: A police car blocking traffic on a main road in a tropical suburban area.

items.title: Border Force busts NT multi-million-dollar illicit tobacco operation

children.content: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

image.altText: A box with black packages with four cigarette packets lined up on top with one pack with cigarettes poking out of one packet

items.title: NT scrutiny committee members call for shake-up after recommendations rejected

children.content: Non-government members of the Northern Territory Legislative Scrutiny Committee are calling for the body to be reformed, after the NT government ignored all 11 recommendations it made to improve a recent bill.

image.altText: A parliamentary committee hears evidence from witnesses

items.title: Finke Desert Race competitors reflect on event ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

articles.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

avatar.alt: Victoria Ellis, a brunette, curly haired woman leans one elbow on a fence and smiles at the camera.

items.title: Israel has invaded deeper into Lebanon using this river crossing

children.content: Images of the first tanks to roll over Lebanon's Litani River were posted online this week. ABC NEWS Verify has found the spot where Israel's military crossed.

articles.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill four

children.content: Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington, and has suggested in recent days that it could intervene directly if Israel keeps up attacks there.

image.altText: Smoke billows from a valley with houses surrounding

items.title: Doctor in tears over 'atypical' moles as social media craze causes concern

children.content: Skin specialists are sounding a warning about the potential cancer risks associated with unregulated peptides at the heart of a social media-driven beauty craze.

image.altText: Large moles on a young person's back.

items.title: Town in shock after couple stabbed and alleged intruder shot

children.content: The Torrington community will meet to discuss security measures following an alleged home invasion and a couple's stabbing.

image.altText: Two elderly people smiling

items.title: PM tells summit economic shocks 'are not passing storms'

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has caused the biggest spike in petrol and diesel prices in history. Follow live.

image.altText: A close-up image of Anthony Albanese looking serious in front of a microphone

items.title: Rumours, rifts and a reported resignation form a murky picture of a war-ravaged Iran

children.content: A report about the resignation of Iran's president earlier this week came at a turbulent moment for Iran's regime, which is grappling with frictions between moderates and hardliners as the war drags on.

image.altText: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian delivers a speech.

items.title: This is the 17th Ebola outbreak in 50 years. But this one is different

children.content: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

image.altText: Young women leans on her kitchen bench, posing for photo

items.title: Qualifier makes French Open history, reaching final against Russian teen star

children.content: Nineteen-year-old Mirra Andreeva reaches her first grand slam final at the French Open where she will take on Poland's Maja Chwalińska, the first qualifier to reach the Paris decider in the Open Era.

image.altText: Maja Chwalinska puts her hand on her head at the French Open.

items.title: ABC news quiz: Mystery markings, a premature celebration and a 'Big' fight

children.content: You too could learn the dangers of celebrating too soon if you underestimate this week's news quiz.

image.altText: Lorena Wiebes holds up her hands

items.title: A new documentary brings things home for the author of Dark Emu

children.content: A slow-cinema treasure, Grace McKenzie's documentary, Yumburra, traces author Bruce Pascoe's establishment of a farm.

image.altText: Man with white hair and beard stands on the front of a small boat on a river, looking thoughtful.

items.title: Looking for another show like Off Campus? Right this way

children.content: Hannah and Garrett's steamy love story might be on ice, but we've found more romance and yearning to tick your comfort-viewing boxes.

items.title: Public servant claims threat of $4m damages bill in golf course fight

children.content: A South Australian public servant taking the state government to court over a controversial redevelopment in the Adelaide Parklands says he will continue to fight the case despite it potentially leading to him losing his house or becoming bankrupt.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Melbourne Storm boss hails 'positive' talks with Tasmania to bring games south

children.content: Melbourne Storm chief executive Justin Rodski says he's "got no doubt" a deal with Tasmania can be reached if the commercial interests line up, following a round of courting from premier Jeremy Rockliff.

image.altText: Cameron Munster of the Storm looks to pass the ball

items.title: Ancient tribes, military, trade and China's rise meet on this island

children.content: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

items.title: Bail matter adjourned for 13yo boy charged with violent extremist offences

children.content: The teenager's bail application has been adjourned until later this month and transferred to the Maryborough Children's Court.

image.altText: A brick building with a sign out the front reading 'Hervey Bay Courthouse'

items.title: Zelenskyy writes open letter inviting Putin to talks to end Ukraine war

children.content: An open letter is the first public message Mr Zelenskyy has written directly to Mr Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

image.altText: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy speaks behind a lectern

items.title: Portugal and Austria defeat Germany for UN Security Council seats

children.content: Portugal and Austria have defeated Germany for seats on the powerful but deeply divided UN Security Council in a hotly contested race after intense campaigning.

image.altText: The symbol of the United Nations - a world map and olive branches - is displayed on the side of high-rise building.

items.title: Experts suspect state policy is putting some farmers in artificial drought

children.content: A western New South Wales farmer says dams built next door have stopped all water flows onto his property, forcing him to graze and water his stock by the side of the road.

articles.title: Farmers are increasingly embracing change, and it's bringing them hope

children.content: When grazier Matthew Peart took over his family's organic beef operation almost 20 years ago, his pastures were rundown, and the future looked bleak. But now, he's unashamedly optimistic, and a new report suggests he's not alone.

image.altText: Mid  shot from behind of farmer leaning backwards against the front of a ute looking out to a sunset

articles.title: Hot spot for Australian bull semen as China market reopens

children.content: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.

image.altText: Photo of vials of frozen bull semen.

items.title: Sex discrimination commissioner slams gender definition proposal

children.content: Australia's sex discrimination commissioner condemns a proposal to reinstate biological definition of sexes as a "step back" for women.

image.altText: Politician speaks in Canberra with Nationals leader in background.

items.title: Socceroo O'Neill shakes off injury scare to be fit for World Cup debut

children.content: Aiden O'Neill put a scare through the Socceroos camp when he went down with an ankle injury in early May, but says he is fit and ready for his World Cup debut.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill of the Socceroos defends against Cameroon.

items.title: 'A lot of confidence' for Ash Gardner ahead of T20 Cup

children.content: Ash Gardner will hit Australia's Twenty20 World Cup warm-up matches full of confidence after a half-century in the last of three informal hit-outs against South Africa.

image.altText: Batter hits the ball off her pads into the legside

items.title: Bullock says RBA will re-tender KPMG whistleblower hotline contract amid scandals

children.content: The Reserve Bank is distancing itself from KPMG with Governor Michele Bullock confirming the bank will re-tender its whistleblower hotline service.

image.altText: A dark-haired woman in glasses casts her gaze downward as she stands at a lectern in forrnt of an RBA backdrop.

items.title: Tantric workshops, couple swaps and toys: Intimacy pressures on MAFS exposed

children.content: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.
